tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Cape Verde's Fearless Draw: 500,000-Strong Island Nation Stuns Two-Time Champions
stories.summary: Cape Verde's 500,000 people stunned two-time champion Uruguay with midfielder Telmo Arcanjo's epic solo run and Kevin Pina's free-kick goal in a thrilling 2-2 draw.

stories.headline: Wedding at the Shelter: Community Celebrates Couple's Journey from Homelessness
stories.summary: Abdul and Teresa Broner married at the church shelter where they once stayed after losing everything, now both driving city buses for Tempe.

stories.headline: From Poverty to Glory: Goalkeeper's Seven Saves Make Him Iran's National Hero
stories.summary: Alireza Beiranvand, who escaped rural homelessness, made seven stunning saves to earn Iran a 0-0 draw against Belgium, becoming his nation's hero.

stories.headline: From Shelter to Home: Police and Lions Club Give Elderly Woman Her First Real House
stories.summary: Gowrajji, a woman in her seventies, walked into her own home for the first time after decades in a makeshift shelter of wooden poles and plastic.

stories.headline: Stem Cells Deliver 15-Year Reprieve: Autoimmune Disease Patients Live Symptom-Free Lives
stories.summary: A man and woman with a devastating autoimmune disease lived symptom-free for over 15 years after stem-cell transplants; he had two children and resumed normal life.

stories.headline: Community Shows Up: Strangers Rally to Save Retired Nurse's Home
stories.summary: Dozens of Northeast Ohio volunteers, including special education teacher Melanie Parker and lawn care pro Norburt Sanek, rallied to save Beverly Thomas's yard from court.

stories.headline: Sturgeon Swims Home: Extinct Species Spawns Wild Again in China's Yangtze
stories.summary: On April 14, Yangtze sturgeon—an ancient species declared extinct in 2022—spawned naturally and successfully hatched without human intervention for the first time.

stories.headline: Wyndham Clark Wins U.S. Open: Triumph Over Heckling, Father's Heart Surgery on Father's Day
stories.summary: Wyndham Clark won the U.S. Open at Shinnecock Hills despite hostile crowds and a shrinking lead, with mental coach Julie Elion helping him overcome his past.

stories.headline: Recruiter's Fare Money Sparks Joy: Unemployed Man Lands Job, Inspires Thousands Online
stories.summary: A recruiter sent interview fare money to a struggling man, sparking a wave of kindness that landed him a job and moved hundreds.

stories.headline: ALBA Berlin Erases 20-Point Deficit to Win Championship in Historic Comeback
stories.summary: Berlin's basketball team stormed back from 20 down at halftime to claim their first championship since 2022 in one of sports' greatest comebacks.

stories.headline: Brain-Computer Interface Restores Voice: First Wireless Implant Helps Patients Speak Again
stories.summary: A Michigan woman with motor neuron disease received the first wireless brain-computer implant, the Connexus BCI, to restore her ability to speak and communicate.

stories.headline: Little Wooden Boxes, Big Joy: Boy's Trading Posts Spread Kindness Across Oklahoma
stories.summary: A Sand Springs boy's handmade wooden boxes of stickers, keychains, and notes have become a beloved community tradition spreading joy across three Oklahoma towns.

stories.headline: HIV Hope: New Vaccines Boost Immune Response in 85% of Trial Participants
stories.summary: Experimental HIV vaccines proved safe and gave robust T-cell responses, offering genuine hope for a curative approach against the virus.

stories.headline: Grade 6 Students Release 93 Salmon: Young Hands Restore Species to Lake Ontario
stories.summary: Joyful students released Atlantic salmon into Duffins Creek, joining efforts to bring the species back to Lake Ontario.

stories.headline: Rare Butterfly Thrives: Kent Hits Record 591 Duke of Burgundy Following Conservation Work
stories.summary: Dedicated farmers, volunteers, and wildflower restoration efforts bring the rare Duke of Burgundy butterfly population to a historic high in Kent.
